Three statements are followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. You have to consider these statements to be true, even if they seem at variance from commonly known facts. Decide which of the given conclusions logically follow/s from the given statements.
Statements:
1. All squigs are brooks.
2. Some brooks are crooks.
3. No crook is top.
Conclusions:
I. Some tops are brooks.
II. No squig is top.
III. Some brooks are squigs.
o: Only conclusions II and III follow.
Only conclusions I and II follow.

Three statements are followed by four conclusions numbered I, II, III and IV. You have to consider these statements to be true, even if they seem at variance from commonly known facts. Decide which of the given conclusions logically follow/s from the given statements.
Statements:
1. Some cucutious are persicus.
2. All persicus are sunflowers.
3. Some sunflowers are cucutious.
Conclusions:
I. Some sunflowers are cucutious.
II. All cucutious are sunflowers.
III. All persicus are cucutious.
IV. No sunflower is cucutious.
o: Only conclusions II and III follow.
Only conclusions I and IV follow.
